edges.description: Activating HRAS variants establish persistent downstream Ras pathway activity.
Persistent HRAS activation perturbs AMPK-linked mitochondrial homeostasis in cardiac tissues.
Overactive HRAS redirects atrial cardiomyocyte identity toward a nodal-like program.
Hyperactive HRAS drives abnormal glucose handling, autophagic flux, and energetic inefficiency in patient fibroblasts.
edges.target: Fibroblast metabolic rewiring and increased energetic expenditure

edges.description: Disease fibroblasts show defective extracellular matrix assembly compatible with an HRAS-linked connective tissue mechanism.
Persistent HRAS pathway activation disrupts the balance of neurogenesis and gliogenesis during brain development.
Constitutive oncogenic HRAS signaling creates lifelong neoplastic susceptibility.
Cardiac bioenergetic stress and impaired mitochondrial homeostasis contribute to ventricular hypertrophic remodeling.
Nodal-like transcriptional reprogramming increases pacemaker-like electrophysiology in atrial cells.
edges.target: Enhanced automaticity and funny current in atrial cardiomyocytes

edges.description: Increased atrial automaticity and pacemaker-like activity manifest clinically as multifocal atrial tachycardia.
edges.source: Enhanced automaticity and funny current in atrial cardiomyocytes

edges.description: Increased energetic expenditure despite adequate intake contributes to poor weight gain.
edges.source: Fibroblast metabolic rewiring and increased energetic expenditure

edges.description: Chronic inefficiency of growth-supporting metabolism contributes to postnatal growth deficiency.
edges.source: Fibroblast metabolic rewiring and increased energetic expenditure

edges.description: Abnormal extracellular matrix assembly likely contributes to the characteristic connective tissue component of facial dysmorphology.
Reduced elastogenesis provides a plausible connective tissue basis for ligamentous laxity.
Abnormal cortical development and neuroglial imbalance contribute to persistent cognitive impairment.
Persistent oncogenic HRAS signaling manifests clinically as increased tumor risk.

nodes.description: HRAS-mutant atrial-like cardiomyocytes acquire a pacemaker-nodal-like gene expression program, including increased ISL1, TBX3, and TBX18 expression, shifting atrial cells toward an arrhythmogenic identity state.

evidence.reference_title: HRAS-Mutant Cardiomyocyte Model of Multifocal Atrial Tachycardia.
evidence.snippet: Mutant ACMs demonstrated elevated gene expression (ie, ISL1, TBX3, TBX18) related to intracellular calcium homeostasis, heart rate, RAS signaling, and induction of pacemaker-nodal-like transcriptional programming.

nodes.description: Costello syndrome models show impaired mitochondrial proteostasis and oxidative phosphorylation in cardiac tissue, indicating that the disease is not solely a surface signaling disorder but also a bioenergetic one.

evidence.snippet: The findings revealed alteration of mitochondrial proteostasis and defective oxidative phosphorylation in the heart and skeletal muscle of CS mice that were also found in the cell models of the disease. The underpinning mechanisms involved the inhibition of the AMPK signaling pathway by mutant forms of HRAS, leading to alteration of mitochondrial proteostasis and bioenergetics.

nodes.description: Distinctive craniofacial appearance with coarse features, full lips, large mouth, and depressed nasal bridge.

evidence.snippet: Costello syndrome is typically characterized by failure to thrive in infancy as a result of severe postnatal feeding difficulties; short stature; developmental delay or intellectual disability; coarse facial features (full lips, large mouth, full nasal tip);

nodes.description: Mutant HRAS remains aberrantly active and persistently engages downstream Ras effector programs, creating a shared upstream driver for cardiac, metabolic, connective tissue, developmental, and oncogenic abnormalities.

evidence.snippet: Germline mutations that activate genes in the canonical RAS/MAPK signaling pathway are responsible for rare human developmental disorders known as RASopathies.

nodes.description: HRAS activation alters the normal balance of neural progenitor expansion, cortical neuron production, and gliogenesis, providing a mechanistic basis for neurodevelopmental impairment.

evidence.snippet: Induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) derived from Costello syndrome showed increased production of cortical neurons associated with extended progenitor phase (Rooney et al., 2016).
During transition from neurogenesis to gliogenesis, Paquin et al. (2009) showed that variants found in Costello syndrome suppress neurogenesis but promote astrogenesis.

nodes.description: Reprogrammed HRAS-mutant atrial cardiomyocytes beat faster, contain more pacemaker-like cells, and show elevated funny current density, providing the proximate electrophysiologic substrate for tachyarrhythmia.
nodes.id: Enhanced automaticity and funny current in atrial cardiomyocytes

evidence.snippet: Electrophysiological assessment revealed an increased number of pacemaker-like cells with elevated funny current densities among mutant ACMs.

nodes.description: Severe feeding difficulties and failure to thrive in infancy, often requiring tube feeding.

evidence.snippet: Costello syndrome is typically characterized by failure to thrive in infancy as a result of severe postnatal feeding difficulties;

In CS, poor weight gain and growth are not caused by low caloric intake. Here, we show that constitutive plasma membrane translocation and activation of the GLUT4 glucose transporter, via reactive oxygen species-dependent AMP-activated protein kinase α and p38 hyperactivation, occurs in primary fibroblasts of CS patients, resulting in accelerated glycolysis and increased fatty acid synthesis and storage as lipid droplets.

nodes.description: Patient fibroblasts show HRAS-driven metabolic dysregulation with abnormal glucose transporter activation, accelerated glycolysis, increased fatty acid synthesis and storage, and accelerated autophagic flux, together consistent with increased resting energetic expenditure.
nodes.id: Fibroblast metabolic rewiring and increased energetic expenditure

evidence.snippet: In CS, poor weight gain and growth are not caused by low caloric intake. Here, we show that constitutive plasma membrane translocation and activation of the GLUT4 glucose transporter, via reactive oxygen species-dependent AMP-activated protein kinase α and p38 hyperactivation, occurs in primary fibroblasts of CS patients, resulting in accelerated glycolysis and increased fatty acid synthesis and storage as lipid droplets. An accelerated autophagic flux was also identified as contributing to the increased energetic expenditure in CS.

nodes.description: Heterozygous germline activating variants in HRAS initiate Costello syndrome and create the constitutive signaling state that drives its multisystem phenotype.

evidence.snippet: These results confirm that CS is caused, in most cases, by heterozygous missense mutations in the proto-oncogene HRAS.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlation in Costello syndrome: HRAS mutation analysis in 43 cases.
evidence.snippet: These results confirm that CS is caused, in most cases, by heterozygous missense mutations in the proto-oncogene HRAS.
HRAS | HGNC:5173 | Costello syndrome | MONDO:0009026 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Cardiac involvement is common, and hypertrophic cardiomyopathy is a major cause of morbidity in Costello syndrome.

evidence.snippet: Cardiac hypertrophy was reported in 34%, which involved the left ventricle in 50% and was usually consistent with classic hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM).

nodes.description: Costello syndrome fibroblasts fail to assemble elastic fibers efficiently, consistent with a connective tissue mechanism involving reduced elastin deposition and abnormal extracellular matrix organization.

evidence.snippet: We found that impaired production of elastic fibers by these fibroblasts is associated with a functional deficiency of the 67-kD elastin-binding protein (EBP), which is normally required to chaperone tropoelastin through the secretory pathways and to its extracellular assembly.

nodes.description: Variable intellectual disability, typically mild to moderate.

evidence.snippet: An evaluation of 15 adult patients 18-32 years of age revealed that 12 had moderate to severe intellectual disability and most live at home without constant medical care.
diffuse hypotonia and joint laxity with ulnar deviation of the wrists and fingers; tight Achilles tendons;

nodes.description: During early childhood, approximately 50% of patients develop multifocal atrial tachycardia (MAT), a treatment-resistant tachyarrhythmia caused by HRAS-driven pacemaker-nodal transcriptional reprogramming in atrial cardiomyocytes.

evidence.reference_title: HRAS-Mutant Cardiomyocyte Model of Multifocal Atrial Tachycardia.
evidence.snippet: During early childhood, 50% of patients develop multifocal atrial tachycardia, a treatment-resistant tachyarrhythmia of unknown pathogenesis.
and arrhythmia (usually supraventricular tachycardia, especially abnormal atrial rhythm / multifocal atrial tachycardia or ectopic atrial tachycardia).

nodes.description: Increased risk of malignancies including rhabdomyosarcoma, neuroblastoma, and bladder carcinoma. Lifetime cancer risk estimated at 15-17%.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlation in Costello syndrome: HRAS mutation analysis in 43 cases.
evidence.snippet: Individuals with CS have an increased risk of malignancy, suggested to be about 17%.

Individuals with Costello syndrome have an approximately 15% lifetime risk for malignant tumors including rhabdomyosarcoma and neuroblastoma in young children and transitional cell carcinoma of the bladder in adolescents and young adults.

nodes.description: Postnatal growth deficiency leading to short stature.

evidence.snippet: Costello syndrome is typically characterized by failure to thrive in infancy as a result of severe postnatal feeding difficulties; short stature;

nodes.description: The same activating HRAS signaling architecture that causes developmental disease also creates susceptibility to embryonal and later-onset malignant neoplasms.

evidence.snippet: Individuals with Costello syndrome have an approximately 15% lifetime risk for malignant tumors including rhabdomyosarcoma and neuroblastoma in young children and transitional cell carcinoma of the bladder in adolescents and young adults.
